seed.title: GLP-1 appetite shock
seed.prompt: Given the thesis that GLP-1 drugs durably reduce appetite and food noise for a large population, generate consequences and select under explore and proof.
seed.thesis: GLP-1 adoption turns appetite from a fixed consumer substrate into a medically adjustable variable, pressuring food volume, snacks, alcohol, apparel sizing, and chronic-disease economics.
goals: separate event disappearance from simple demand decline

operators.label: Substrate removal
operators.description: Find the removed consumer or institutional substrate.

operators.label: Hidden dependent
operators.description: Find businesses that depend on the substrate continuing.

operators.label: Adoption boundary
operators.description: Look for uneven adoption or access edges.

sourcePackets.title: Snack occasion removal
sourcePackets.mechanism: If food noise falls, the profitable impulse occasion disappears rather than merely becoming cheaper.

candidate.title: Snack shelf loses the impulse event
candidate.thesis: GLP-1 pressure hits snacks hardest where profit depends on unplanned eating occasions, not staple calories.

delta.summary: Moved from weight loss to disappearance of impulse eating events.

claims: Impulse snacks monetize food noise and unplanned occasions.
Staples should prove more resilient than margin-rich impulse categories.

sourcePackets.title: Alcohol craving link
sourcePackets.mechanism: If GLP-1s dampen reward-seeking, alcohol and other craving-driven categories face demand pressure outside calorie math.

candidate.title: Alcohol gets hit through craving, not calories
candidate.thesis: The larger GLP-1 branch may be reward-loop dampening, where alcohol demand falls because craving quiets, not because drink calories are counted.

delta.summary: Moved from appetite to reward-loop consumption.

claims: Craving reduction would affect alcohol even when food volume is not the direct mechanism.
The claim is checkable through category spend and clinical addiction signals.

sourcePackets.title: Access boundary
sourcePackets.mechanism: Uneven GLP-1 access creates visible consumer splits between covered, cash-pay, compounded, and excluded groups.

candidate.title: Appetite becomes an access class
candidate.thesis: GLP-1 adoption turns appetite suppression into an access class, splitting consumer behavior by coverage, cash-pay capacity, and supply reliability.

delta.summary: Moved from average weight-loss adoption to uneven access as the market edge.

claims: Consumer spend shifts unevenly if only some groups sustain access.
The boundary between covered and uncovered users becomes a market signal.

sourcePackets.title: People lose weight
sourcePackets.mechanism: Restates the seed as weight loss without a new removed event or dependent.
sourcePackets.noDeltaReason: No new substrate beyond the GLP-1 weight-loss thesis.

sourcePackets.title: Checkout aisle repricing
sourcePackets.mechanism: If impulse occasions vanish, the checkout aisle changes from high-margin snack conversion into pickup, pharmacy, or loyalty real estate.

candidate.title: Checkout shelf becomes loyalty real estate
candidate.thesis: The snack branch gets sharper at the checkout shelf: GLP-1s weaken the impulse slot, so retailers reprice that space toward pharmacy pickup, loyalty, or prepared essentials.

delta.summary: Moved from snack volume to the physical retail shelf whose economics depend on impulse.

claims: Checkout snacks monetize unplanned craving at the last moment.
Retailers can observe the shift through basket mix and shelf yield.
